title: Honor preregistration as source-of-truth; raw categories stay distinct
context: Audit of EXP-03 revision-2 against immutable preregistration (expreg_ba6aef4ecd) found three violations: cannot_decide folded into uncompilable, abandoned/timeout pathways blocked, raw decision counts hidden.
decision: System must track and report exactly what preregistration specifies. Raw review outcomes (cannot_decide) are distinct enum tokens, never reinterpreted or folded. Non-answer terminal states (abandoned/timeout) flow through raw submission path. All preregistered decision tokens appear in reports with zero-filling for visibility.
consequences: Enum vocabulary is now immutable—cannot_decide is its own category, preserving what reviewer actually chose vs. code compilation outcome
Terminal non-answers (abandoned/timeout) bypass template-only restriction, staying bookkeepable as preregistration requires
Arm reports expose preregistered raw count per decision token; aggregation hidden from downstream
Changes are additive—revision-1 records and enum values untouched, no breaking schema migration
alternatives_rejected: Continue mapping cannot_decide→uncompilable (loses fidelity to preregistration; obscures what reviewer recorded)
Block abandoned/timeout entirely (violates preregistration's terminal-state bookkeeping requirement)
Omit or aggregate raw counts from reports (defeats preregistration transparency—no visibility into outcome distribution)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Continue mapping cannot_decide→uncompilable (loses fidelity to preregistration; obscures what reviewer recorded)
Block abandoned/timeout entirely (violates preregistration's terminal-state bookkeeping requirement)
Omit or aggregate raw counts from reports (defeats preregistration transparency—no visibility into outcome distribution)
